Home.heroDesc: Specialized language training built around clinical settings — by a registered nurse who learned Finnish and now works in Finnish A&E.
Home.who1Body: Nurses, doctors, physiotherapists, and other health professionals working or planning to work in Finland.
Home.who2Body: You already have general Finnish. These courses sharpen your clinical and professional language for real workplace situations.
Home.who3Body: Trained abroad and navigating the Finnish healthcare system. Bridging language gaps with real clinical context.
Home.why1Body: Rachael works in Accident & Emergency in Finland. Every lesson is grounded in situations she encounters daily — not textbook scenarios.
Home.why2Body: Rachael is not a native Finnish speaker. She learned through courses and self-study, holds certificates from JAMK and Helsinki University, and understands the challenges you face.
Home.why3Body: Elderly care, geriatric, medical, cardiac, and emergency wards. The vocabulary spans the full spectrum of hospital care.
Home.quote: I know what it feels like to struggle with Finnish in a clinical setting. That's exactly why I teach it.
Home.coursesSub: Online — flexible scheduling around your shifts.
Home.how1Body: Browse group or 1-on-1 options and pick the schedule that fits your shifts.
Home.how2Body: Fill in the form. Rachael confirms your place and sends you a schedule within 3 working days.
Home.how3Body: Online sessions with real clinical scenarios, vocabulary, and role-play.
Home.how4Body: Use Finnish fluently in your ward — with patients, colleagues, and in documentation.

About.heroDesc: I'm a registered nurse working in Accident & Emergency in Finland. I teach healthcare Finnish because I've lived the experience of learning it — and I know exactly where the gaps are between textbook Finnish and real clinical communication.
About.bgP1: I graduated in 2005 from JAMK University College of Health and Social Sciences. Since then I've worked across a wide range of healthcare settings in Finland — from elderly care homes and geriatric wards to cardiac units and, primarily, Accident & Emergency.
About.bgP2: I didn't grow up speaking Finnish. I learned it the hard way — through formal courses, self-study, and the daily necessity of working in a Finnish-speaking environment. I hold language certificates from JAMK and Helsinki University.
About.bgP3: That experience gave me a very specific perspective: I know which Finnish phrases you actually need in a resus bay. I know how patients describe pain, confusion, and distress. And I know how nurses and doctors communicate in handovers, documentation, and emergencies.
About.bgP4: I teach healthcare Finnish to health professionals who already have general Finnish (B2 or above) and want to apply it with confidence in clinical settings.
About.clinical1Body: Primary department. High-pressure, fast-paced communication with patients, families, and multidisciplinary teams.
About.clinical2Body: Vocabulary for cardiac monitoring, medications, patient education, and clinical documentation.
About.clinical3Body: General ward communication, medication rounds, and interdisciplinary handovers.
About.clinical4Body: Patient-centred communication with older adults, cognitive assessment language, and family discussions.
About.clinical5Body: Community-based care vocabulary, daily care documentation, and resident interaction.
About.clinical6Body: Triage language, ABCDE assessment terminology, and emergency team communication.
About.qual4Sub: Active clinical practice in Finnish healthcare
About.philQuote: Healthcare Finnish is not about being perfect. It's about being understood — by your patient, your colleague, and yourself in a high-pressure moment.
About.philBody: Every session is built around realistic situations. Not grammar drills. Not abstract vocabulary lists. Real language, in real contexts, that you'll use on your next shift.

Courses.heroDesc: Specialized healthcare Finnish for professionals with B2 level or above. All sessions run online and are scheduled around your working hours.
Courses.groupDesc: Learn alongside other healthcare professionals in small online groups. Real scenarios, shared vocabulary building, and peer discussion — a great way to stay consistent and motivated.
Courses.starterDesc: A focused introduction to healthcare Finnish in a group setting. Ideal for trialling the format before committing to more.
Courses.fullDesc: The comprehensive programme covering all major clinical areas: A&E, ward care, documentation, and patient interaction.
Courses.individualDesc: Fully personalised sessions built around your specialty and your gaps. Rachael tailors each lesson to your department — whether that's emergency care, geriatrics, surgical wards, or community nursing.
Courses.iFeature1: Tailored to your specialty and department
Courses.iFeature2: Flexible — book at times that suit your shifts
Courses.iFeature3: Fast, direct feedback on pronunciation and phrasing
Courses.iFeature4: Focus on areas you find hardest
Courses.iFeature5: Real scenarios from Rachael's clinical practice
Courses.indCardDesc: Buy single sessions or a block. No minimum commitment — start with one and continue when it works for you.
Courses.pdfDesc: Clinical vocabulary sheets, phrase guides, and documentation templates. Organised by department and topic.
Courses.pdfPrice: Priced per pack. Get in touch for details.
Courses.videoDesc: Pre-recorded lessons covering core healthcare Finnish topics. Watch and rewatch at your own pace — ideal for shift workers.
Courses.videoSub: Annual subscription fee. Join the waitlist to be notified when it launches.
Courses.faq1Q: What level of Finnish do I need?
Courses.faq1A: These courses are designed for professionals who already have B2-level Finnish or above. If you're unsure whether you qualify, book a free call with Rachael and she'll advise you.
Courses.faq2A: All sessions are online via video call (Zoom or Teams). You just need a stable internet connection and a quiet space.
Courses.faq3Q: Can I do both group and 1-on-1?
Courses.faq3A: Absolutely. Many students do group sessions for general clinical vocabulary and add 1-on-1 sessions to focus on their specific department or gaps.
Courses.faq4A: I'll get in touch within 3 working days to arrange a short intro call, confirm your background, and agree on a schedule.

Contact.heroDesc: Whether you want to sign up, ask a question, or just find out if these courses are right for you — I'd love to hear from you. I will respond in 3 working days.
Contact.responseValue: I will respond in 3 working days

Contact.next3: I'll send you a schedule and payment details.
Contact.next4: You're in — your first session is booked.
Contact.optCall: Just a free intro call first
